title: scMMR — Single-Cell Multi-Task Model in R
text: scMMR 是一个基于 PyTorch 深度学习的 R 包，通过 reticulate 桥接 R 与 Python。核心功能包括： - 多任务 DNN：联合学习细胞类型分类 + UMAP 嵌入回归（共享 ResNet 骨干） - Bulk 反卷积：从 scRNA-seq 参考训练 DNN，估计 bulk RNA-seq 中的细胞类型比例 - 可解释性：Integrated Gradients 基因/通路重要性分析 - 下游分析：差异丰度检验、扰动排名、通路分析、基因集评分、CytoTRACE2 版本: 0.2.0 作者: Yuhao Ouyang License: MIT

what: multi_task_model.py — 多任务 DNN (1,490 行)
title: scMMR — Single-Cell Multi-Task Model in R
text: 关键特性： - GradNorm 多任务损失平衡 - Label smoothing 正则化 - OOD 检测 (置信度阈值) - Integrated Gradients 可解释性 - CosineAnnealingWarmRestarts 学习率调度 - 支持 fine-tune 和 transfer learning
code: 输入 (binary: X > 0)   ↓ InputBlock: BatchNorm → Dropout(0.25) → Linear(n_genes → 512) → SELU   ↓ ResNetBlock ×4: Dropout(0.1) → Linear(512 → 512) → SELU + skip connection   ↓ ResNetLastBlock: Dropout(0.1) → Linear(512 → 512) → SELU (无 skip)   ↓ (512-dim shared embedding)   ├─→ ClassificationHead: Linear(512→256) → SELU → Dropout → Linear(256→K)   └─→ RegressionHead: Linear(512→256) → SELU → Dropout → Linear(256→D)

what: deconv_model.py — 反卷积 DNN (587 行)
title: scMMR — Single-Cell Multi-Task Model in R
text: 关键差异 (vs 多任务 DNN)： - 输入：连续 log-CPM（不是二值化 X > 0） - 输出：细胞类型比例向量（softmax 归一化） - 损失：MSE 或 KL 散度 - 训练数据：Dirichlet(α=1) 采样生成的 pseudo-bulk
code: 输入 (continuous: log1p(CPM))   ↓ InputBlock → ResNetBlock ×4 → ResNetLastBlock   ↓ (512-dim embedding)   ↓ TaskHead: Linear(512→256) → SELU → Dropout → Linear(256→K)   ↓ Softmax → proportions (sum = 1)

title: scMMR — Single-Cell Multi-Task Model in R
text: 输入灵活性：所有核心函数同时接受 h5ad 路径和 Seurat 对象 S3 泛型：ComputeModuleScore() 和 RunCytoTRACE2() 支持 matrix/Seurat 分发 Seurat v5 兼容：写入用 AddMetaData()，读取用 @meta.data[[col]] msigdbr API：使用 collection（非 category），subcollection（非 subcategory），CP:KEGG_LEGACY（非 CP:KEGG） reticulate 返回值：Python 对象用 reticulate::py_to_r() 转换，list 元素用 [["key"]] 双括号提取（避免 $ 与 Python 对象冲突） 整数参数：传给 Python 的整数必须用 .integer() 或 L 后缀 Roxygen 标签：导出函数用 @export，不用 @keywords internal Collate 顺序：DESCRIPTION 中按依赖顺序排列（zzz → python_env → utils → … ）

title: scMMR — Single-Cell Multi-Task Model in R
text: 模块复用：DeconvModel 复用 multi_task_model.py 的 InputBlock、ResNetBlock、TaskHead 桥接函数命名：多任务用 mt_*，反卷积用 deconv_* 返回值：所有桥接函数返回 plain dict/numpy（不返回 PyTorch tensor 或 Python class） 设备管理：全局变量 _DEVICE 通过 set_device() 设置，所有函数共享 稀疏矩阵：自动检测 scipy.sparse，必要时 .toarray()

title: Single-Cell Multi-Task Model in R
text: scMMR trains shared ResNet backbone jointly learns cell type classification embedding regression (e.g., UMAP coordinates), producing unified 512-dimensional cell representation. Built top representation, scMMR provides: Cell type prediction OOD (--distribution) detection Bulk RNA-seq deconvolution cell type proportion estimation Gene importance attribution via Integrated Gradients Pathway enrichment analysis (GSEA & SCPA) 16 bundled GMT databases Perturbation ranking using Wasserstein, MMD, energy distance metrics Pseudotime trajectory analysis CytoTRACE 2 integration Quality control including doublet detection ambient RNA estimation Rich visualization suite (alluvial, bubble, scatter, beeswarm, GSEA plots, )

title: Single-Cell Multi-Task Model in R
text: Multi-task loss balanced dynamically via GradNorm.
code: Binary Input (gene × cell)   ↓ InputBlock (BatchNorm → Dropout → Linear 512 → SELU)   ↓ ResNetBlock ×4 (512-dim with skip connections)   ↓ Shared 512-dim embedding   ├→ ClassificationHead → K cell types (with label smoothing)   └→ RegressionHead → D embedding dims (e.g., UMAP)

title: Calculate gene module scores — ComputeModuleScore
text: x gene expression matrix (genes x cells) Seurat object. ... Additional arguments passed scoring methods. gene.sets Gene sets one three formats: named list character vectors (gene names). data.frame least two columns: term (gene set name) gene (gene symbol). file path GMT file. method Scoring method: "AUCell" (default), "Seurat", "UCell". min.size Minimum number genes (filtering) gene set scored. Default: 20. batch.size Number cells per batch AUCell method reduce memory. Default: 500. nbin Number expression bins Seurat method control gene selection. Default: 24. ctrl Number control genes per feature gene Seurat method. Default: 100. cores Number parallel cores. Default: 1. seed Random seed reproducibility. Default: 11. assay Name Seurat assay use. Default: DefaultAssay(x). layer Data layer extract: "counts" "data". Defaults "counts" AUCell/UCell "data" Seurat method. store Storage destination scores: "assay" (default) stores new assay, "metadata" stores columns meta.data. assay.name Name new assay store = "assay". Default: method name. prefix Column name prefix store = "metadata". Default: method name followed underscore (e.g. "AUCell_setA").

title: Predict Cell Type Proportions from Bulk RNA-seq — DNN_deconv_predict
text: bulk_expr Bulk expression data. Accepts multiple formats: matrix data.frame (genes x samples).     Rownames = gene names, colnames = sample names. CSV file path (genes x samples, first column =     gene names rownames). h5ad file path. Seurat object (uses RNA assay counts). model_path Path trained deconvolution model (.pt file created DNN_deconv_train). device Compute device: "auto" (default), "cpu", "cuda". adaptive Logical; TRUE, run TAPE-style adaptive stage refine proportions extract cell-type-specific GEP (default FALSE). adaptive_mode Adaptive mode: "overall" (default) adapts samples jointly (one shared GEP), "high-resolution" adapts per-sample (individual GEPs, slower). adaptive_max_iter Number alternating decoder/encoder rounds (default 3). adaptive_steps Gradient steps per phase per round (default 300). adaptive_lr Learning rate adaptive optimization (default 1e-4).

title: Predict Cell Type Proportions from Bulk RNA-seq — DNN_deconv_predict
text: adaptive = FALSE: data.frame   sample_id one column per cell type (backward compatible). adaptive = TRUE: named list : proportions data.frame cell type proportions. sigmatrix Cell-type-specific GEP.       "overall" mode: data.frame (cell_types x genes).       "high-resolution" mode: 3D array       (samples x cell_types x genes).

title: Predict Cell Type Proportions from Bulk RNA-seq — DNN_deconv_predict
text: function handles gene alignment automatically: genes present bulk data model used; missing genes zero-filled. Expression normalized log1p(CPM) match training procedure. adaptive = TRUE, model runs TAPE-style adaptive stage alternately fine-tunes decoder encoder target bulk data, producing tissue-adapted cell-type-specific gene expression profiles (GEP / sigmatrix).

what: Predict Cell Types Using a Trained Multi-Task DNN Model — DNN_predict
title: Predict Cell Types Using a Trained Multi-Task DNN Model — DNN_predict
text: Loads trained multi-task model, runs predictions query data, optionally computes gene importance via Integrated Gradients. GMT file provided, per-cell pathway importance scores also computed aggregating gene-level IG attributions pathways.

title: Predict Cell Types Using a Trained Multi-Task DNN Model — DNN_predict
code: DNN_predict(   query,   model_path,   save_path = NULL,   true_label_col = "cell_type",   explain = FALSE,   top_k_global = 15L,   top_k_class = 10L,   n_cells_explain = 50L,   pathway_gmt = NULL,   pathway_min_genes = 5L,   pathway_n_steps = 20L,   return_embedding = FALSE,   device = "auto" )

title: Predict Cell Types Using a Trained Multi-Task DNN Model — DNN_predict
text: query Path h5ad file Seurat object containing query single-cell data. model_path Path saved model file (.pt). save_path Path save results HDF5 (.h5). Set NULL skip saving. true_label_col Column obs ground-truth labels accuracy evaluation. Set NULL labels available. explain Logical. TRUE, compute gene importance via Integrated Gradients (default FALSE). top_k_global Number top genes global importance (default 15). top_k_class Number top genes per cell type (default 10). n_cells_explain Number cells attribution baseline (speed tradeoff, default 50). pathway_gmt Path GMT file pathway-level scoring. Requires explain = TRUE. provided, computes per-cell pathway importance via |IG attributions| × GMT mask / pathway_size. Built-GMT files available package (system.file("extdata/gmt", package = "scMMR")): reactome.gmt, GO_bp.gmt, TF.gmt, immune.gmt (human), plus m_reactome.gmt, m_GO_bp.gmt, m_TF.gmt (mouse). Set NULL skip pathway scoring. pathway_min_genes Minimum number overlapping genes pathway model's variable genes pathway retained (default 5). pathway_n_steps Number Integrated Gradients interpolation steps pathway scoring (default 20). Lower values faster less precise. gene importance n_steps=50 used; pathway scoring tolerates fewer steps since attributions aggregated across many genes. return_embedding Logical. TRUE, include 512-dim shared embedding output (default FALSE). device Compute device: "auto" (default), "cpu", "cuda".

title: Predict Cell Types Using a Trained Multi-Task DNN Model — DNN_predict
text: named list : predictions data.frame columns: cell_id, cell_type_pred,           confidence, is_ood, umap_1_pred, umap_2_pred.           cell_type_pred always contains model's best-guess           label (never "Unknown"). Use is_ood           confidence downstream filtering. shared_embedding Matrix shape (n_cells, 512) — shared           representation model backbone. included           return_embedding = TRUE. imp_global data.frame (gene, importance) explain=TRUE,           otherwise NULL. Computed via Integrated Gradients. imp_per_class data.frame (cell_type, n_cells, rank, gene,           importance) explain=TRUE, otherwise NULL. pathway_scores Matrix shape (n_cells, n_pathways)           per-cell pathway importance scores. Rownames cell IDs;           column names pathway names. included           pathway_gmt provided explain = TRUE.           Scores represent pathway's discriminative contribution           (via Integrated Gradients), expression enrichment.

title: Predict Cell Types Using a Trained Multi-Task DNN Model — DNN_predict
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ library(scMMR) use_scMMR_python(condaenv = "scanpy-env")  # Basic prediction pred <- DNN_predict(   query      = "query.h5ad",   model_path = "models/my_model.pt",   explain    = TRUE,   device     = "cpu" ) head(pred$predictions) pred$imp_global  # With pathway scoring (GMT file) gmt <- system.file("extdata/gmt", "reactome.gmt", package = "scMMR") pred2 <- DNN_predict(   query       = "query.h5ad",   model_path  = "models/my_model.pt",   explain     = TRUE,   pathway_gmt = gmt,   device      = "cpu" ) dim(pred2$pathway_scores)  # n_cells x n_pathways } # }

what: Train a Multi-Task DNN Model — DNN_train
title: Train a Multi-Task DNN Model — DNN_train
text: One-step function loads reference data, selects highly variable genes, creates multi-task neural network model, trains , configures OOD confidence threshold, saves trained model. model jointly learns cell type classification embedding (e.g. UMAP) regression.

title: Train a Multi-Task DNN Model — DNN_train
code: DNN_train(   input,   label_column,   embedding_key,   save_path = "model.pt",   n_top_genes = 6000L,   batch_key = NULL,   ood_threshold = 0.5,   hidden_size = 512L,   num_epochs = 50L,   learning_rate = 0.001,   batch_size = 256L,   device = "auto",   head_hidden_size = 256L,   n_resnet_blocks = 4L,   input_dropout_rate = 0.25,   resnet_dropout_rate = 0.1,   head_dropout_rate = 0.1,   test_size = 0.2,   early_stopping_patience = 10L,   early_stopping_monitor = "cls",   use_gradnorm = TRUE,   gradnorm_alpha = 0.15,   label_smoothing = 0.1,   use_lr_scheduler = TRUE,   use_uncertainty = FALSE,   random_state = 42L )

title: Train a Multi-Task DNN Model — DNN_train
text: input Path h5ad file Seurat object containing reference single-cell data. Must include cell type labels obs embedding coordinates obsm. label_column Column name obs cell type labels (e.g. "cell_type"). embedding_key Key obsm embedding coordinates (e.g. "umap", "X_umap"). save_path Path save trained model (.pt file). Parent directories created automatically. n_top_genes Number highly variable genes select (default 6000). batch_key Column obs batch-aware HVG selection. Set NULL batch correction. ood_threshold OOD (--distribution) confidence threshold (default 0.5). Cells confidence flagged is_ood = TRUE predictions. Cell type labels always retain model's best-guess; users can filter is_ood confidence downstream. hidden_size Width shared backbone (default 512). num_epochs Maximum training epochs (default 50). learning_rate Learning rate (default 0.001). batch_size Training batch size (default 256). device Compute device: "auto" (default, uses GPU available), "cpu" (force CPU), "cuda" (require GPU). head_hidden_size Width task-specific heads (default 256). n_resnet_blocks Number ResNet blocks (default 4). input_dropout_rate Dropout rate input layer (default 0.25). resnet_dropout_rate Dropout rate ResNet blocks (default 0.1). head_dropout_rate Dropout rate task heads (default 0.1). test_size Fraction data validation (default 0.2). early_stopping_patience Epochs wait early stopping (default 10). early_stopping_monitor Metric monitor: "cls" "reg" (default "cls"). use_gradnorm Use GradNorm multi-task balancing (default TRUE). gradnorm_alpha GradNorm alpha parameter (default 0.15). label_smoothing Label smoothing factor (default 0.1). use_lr_scheduler Use cosine annealing LR scheduler (default TRUE). use_uncertainty Use uncertainty-aware regression (default FALSE). random_state Random seed (default 42).

title: Find Upstream Regulon Drivers of Pathways — FindRegulonDrivers

title: Volcano-Style Correlation Plot — PlotCorrelation
text: data data.frame RunCorrelation, RunTraceGene, RunTraceGSEA. Must contain name column (gene Pathway), score column (score, rho, Score/NES), p-value column (pvalue/PValue, padj/ padjust/AdjPValue).  Column matching case-insensitive.  optional grouping column (group/ lineage/Lineage) enables faceting. use.padj Logical.  Use adjusted p-value (padj padjust) instead raw pvalue x-axis significance threshold. Default: TRUE. p.cutoff Numeric.  Significance threshold chosen p-value column. Default: 0.05. cor.cutoff Numeric.  Minimum absolute correlation considered significant. Default: 0 (direction). label Character character vector controlling genes label. "top" (default) — label topn genes largest     absolute correlation among significant hits. "sig" — label significant genes. "" — label every point. "none" — labels. character vector specific gene names. topn Integer.  Number top genes label label = "top". Default: 10. col.pos Character. Color significant positive correlations. Default: "#E64B35" (red). col.neg Character. Color significant negative correlations. Default: "#4DBBD5" (blue). col.ns Character. Color non-significant points. Default: "grey70". size.Character. Variable map point size. "none" (default) — points use point.size. "cor" — size mapped absolute correlation value. "pvalue" — size mapped \(-\log_{10}(p)\). point.size Numeric. Fixed point size size.= "none", ignored otherwise. Default: 2.5. size.range Numeric vector length 2. Range point sizes size."none". Default: c(0.5, 5). point.alpha Numeric. Point transparency. Default: 0.7. label.size Numeric. Label text size. Default: 3.5. label.color Character. Label text color. Default: "black". box.padding Numeric. Padding around label boxes (passed ggrepel::geom_text_repel). Default: 0.5. max.overlaps Integer. Maximum overlapping labels. Default: 20. title Character. Plot title. Default: NULL (auto-generated target attribute). xlab Character. X-axis label.  Default: auto. ylab Character. Y-axis label.  Default: NULL (auto: "Correlation" "NES" depending input). ncol Integer.  Number columns group column present faceting used. Default: 3.

title: Volcano-Style Correlation Plot — PlotCorrelation
text: ggplot object.

title: Volcano-Style Correlation Plot — PlotCorrelation
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # --- RunCorrelation output --- res <- RunCorrelation(seu, target = "PTH") PlotCorrelation(res) PlotCorrelation(res, label = "top", topn = 15) PlotCorrelation(res, label = c("GCM2", "CASR", "VDR"))  # Map point size to |correlation| PlotCorrelation(res, size.by = "cor")  # Map point size to -log10(p) PlotCorrelation(res, size.by = "pvalue", size.range = c(1, 6))  # Per-group (RunCorrelation) res <- RunCorrelation(seu, target = "PTH", group.by = "celltype") PlotCorrelation(res, size.by = "cor")  # --- RunTraceGene output (rho + padjust + lineage) --- tg <- RunTraceGene(seu, lineages = c("Lineage1", "Lineage2")) PlotCorrelation(tg)                   # faceted by lineage PlotCorrelation(tg, size.by = "cor")  # size = |rho|  # --- RunTraceGSEA output (Score/NES + AdjPValue + Lineage) --- gsea <- RunTraceGSEA(seu, lineages = c("Lineage1", "Lineage2"),                      gene.sets = gmt) PlotCorrelation(gsea)                 # ylab auto = "NES" PlotCorrelation(gsea, size.by = "pvalue") } # }

what: Plot Dynamic Features Along Pseudotime — PlotDynamicFeatures
title: Plot Dynamic Features Along Pseudotime — PlotDynamicFeatures
text: Visualise gene expression metadata features function pseudotime (continuous trajectory variable). Supports three fitting methods user-controllable smoothness: GAM (mgcv): control smoothness via smooth_k         (smaller = smoother). loess: control smoothness via loess_span         (larger = smoother). B-spline: control smoothness via bspline_knot         (knots = flexible / less smooth).

title: Plot Dynamic Features Along Pseudotime — PlotDynamicFeatures
code: PlotDynamicFeatures(   srt,   pseudotime,   features,   group.by = NULL,   assay = NULL,   layer = "counts",   fit_method = c("gam", "loess", "bspline"),   smooth_k = 10,   loess_span = 0.75,   bspline_knot = 3,   family = NULL,   exp_method = c("log1p", "raw", "zscore"),   lib_normalize = (layer == "counts"),   add_point = TRUE,   add_line = TRUE,   add_interval = TRUE,   add_rug = TRUE,   pt.size = 1,   line.size = 1,   point_palette = "Paired",   point_palcolor = NULL,   line_palette = "Dark2",   line_palcolor = NULL,   compare_lineages = TRUE,   compare_features = FALSE,   ncol = NULL,   nrow = NULL,   reverse = FALSE,   flip = FALSE,   seed = 11,   ... )

title: Plot Dynamic Features Along Pseudotime — PlotDynamicFeatures
text: srt Seurat object. pseudotime Character vector column name(s) srt@meta.data containing pseudotime values. Multiple names enable multi-lineage comparison. features Character vector gene names (looked assay) /column names srt@meta.data. group.Optional character string. column srt@meta.data used colour scatter points. assay Character. Seurat assay use. Default: active assay. layer Character. Data layer use. Default "counts". fit_method Character, one "gam", "loess", "bspline". Default "gam". smooth_k Integer. Number basis dimensions GAM smooth term (mgcv::s(k = ...)). Smaller values produce smoother curves. Default 10. loess_span Numeric (0, 1]. span parameter stats::loess(). Larger values produce smoother curves. Default 0.75. bspline_knot Integer. Number internal knots B-spline fitting. knots allow flexible (less smooth) curve. Default 3. family Character family object GAM. NULL (default), "gaussian" used. exp_method Character, one "log1p", "raw", "zscore". Transformation applied expression values plotting. Default "log1p". lib_normalize Logical. Whether normalise library size. Default TRUE layer = "counts". add_point Logical. Show scatter points. Default TRUE. add_line Logical. Show fitted curve. Default TRUE. add_interval Logical. Show 95% confidence interval ribbon. Default TRUE. add_rug Logical. Show rug marks along x-axis. Default TRUE. pt.size Numeric. Point size. Default 1. line.size Numeric. Line width. Default 1. point_palette Character. Palette name scatter points / rug coloured group.. See show_palettes available names. Default "Paired". point_palcolor Optional character vector custom colours group.variable. Overrides point_palette. line_palette Character. Palette name fitted lines / ribbons. Default "Dark2". line_palcolor Optional character vector custom colours lineages features. Overrides line_palette. compare_lineages Logical. multiple pseudotime columns given, overlay single panel per feature. Default TRUE. compare_features Logical. multiple features given, overlay single panel per lineage. Default FALSE. ncol Integer. Number columns facet layout. nrow Integer. Number rows facet layout. reverse Logical. Reverse pseudotime axis. Default FALSE. flip Logical. Flip x y axes. Default FALSE. seed Integer. Random seed. Default 11. ... Additional arguments passed fitting function (mgcv::gam, stats::loess, stats::lm.fit).

title: Plot GSEA Results — PlotGsea
text: Generates various types plots Gene Set Enrichment Analysis (GSEA) results produced RunGsea.

title: Plot GSEA Results — PlotGsea
code: PlotGsea(   gsea_result,   type = c("line", "comparison", "bar", "lollipop", "ridge", "network", "enrichmap",     "wordcloud", "volcano_nes", "circle", "sankey"),   group_use = NULL,   id_use = NULL,   topTerm = 6,   direction = c("both", "pos", "neg"),   padjustCutoff = 0.05,   line_width = 1.5,   line_color = "#6BB82D",   n_coregene = 0,   features_label = NULL,   label.size = 3.5,   character_width = 50,   word_type = c("term", "feature"),   word_size = c(2, 8),   topWord = 100,   network_layout = "fr",   enrichmap_layout = "fr",   enrichmap_cluster = "fast_greedy",   enrichmap_nlabel = 4,   nes_cutoff = 1,   circ_type = c("c", "h", "m"),   topGenes = 5,   palette = "Spectral",   palcolor = NULL,   combine = TRUE,   ncol = NULL,   nrow = NULL,   seed = 11 )

title: Plot GSEA Results — PlotGsea
text: gsea_result list returned RunGsea, containing enrichment (data.frame), results (named list gseaResult objects), input. type type plot generate. One : "line" Classic 3-panel GSEA running score plot     (enrichment score curve + hit marks + ranked metric).     Requires group_use specify one group. "comparison" NES bubble plot comparing pathways     across groups. "bar" Bidirectional NES bar plot per group. "lollipop" Lollipop chart showing NES     -log10(p.adjust) colour gradient. "ridge" Ridge plot gene set enrichment scores     via enrichplot::ridgeplot(). "network" Gene-pathway network graph (requires     igraph ggrepel). "enrichmap" Enrichment map: pathway nodes connected     shared genes, clustered (requires igraph     ggforce). "wordcloud" Word cloud pathway terms core     enrichment genes (requires ggwordcloud). "volcano_nes" Volcano-style GSEA plot     -log10(p.adjust) x-axis NES y-axis.     Points coloured significance (Activated / Repressed / ns). "circle" Circular GSEA plot showing running score     curves, hit marks ranked-list heatmap circos tracks     (requires circlize). "sankey" Sankey flow diagram linking core enrichment     genes pathways (requires ggsankey). group_use Character vector group names include. type = "line", specify one group. types, defaults available groups. id_use Character vector pathway IDs display. NULL (default), pathways selected automatically topTerm direction. topTerm Number top pathways show per direction. Default 6. direction enrichment direction include: "" (default), "pos", "neg". padjustCutoff Significance threshold filtering pathways. Default 0.05. line_width Line width running score curve. Default 1.5. line_color Color(s) running score lines. Default "#6BB82D". n_coregene Number core enrichment genes label line plot. Default 0 (labels). features_label Character vector specific genes label line plot. Overrides n_coregene. label.size Size gene labels. Default 3.5. character_width Maximum character width wrapping pathway names. Default 50. word_type type = "wordcloud": source words. "term" splits pathway names; "feature" uses core enrichment genes. Default "term". word_size type = "wordcloud": range text sizes. Default c(2, 8). topWord type = "wordcloud": maximum number words display. Default 100. network_layout type = "network": igraph layout algorithm name (e.g., "fr", "kk", "circle"). Default "fr". enrichmap_layout type = "enrichmap": igraph layout algorithm. Default "fr". enrichmap_cluster type = "enrichmap": igraph community detection algorithm (e.g., "fast_greedy", "louvain"). Default "fast_greedy". enrichmap_nlabel type = "enrichmap": number top terms per cluster show labels. Default 4. nes_cutoff type = "volcano_nes": NES threshold significance classification. Default 1. circ_type type = "circle": inner track style. "c" = hit marks + heatmap, "h" = coloured segments + heatmap, "m" = tick marks . Default "c". topGenes type = "sankey": number top core genes show per pathway. Default 5. palette Color palette name fills (passed palette_colors). Default "Spectral". palcolor Custom color vector. Overrides palette. combine Logical; whether combine multiple panels one plot using patchwork. Default TRUE. ncol Number columns combining multiple plots. Default NULL (auto). nrow Number rows combining multiple plots. Default NULL (auto). seed Random seed. Default 11.

title: Plot GSEA Results — PlotGsea
text: ggplot object list ggplot objects.

title: Plot GSEA Results — PlotGsea
text: function dispatches internal helpers depending type: .plot_gsea_line — classic 3-panel GSEA plot using     patchwork. .plot_gsea_comparison — bubble plot NES colour, setSize     size, significance border. .plot_gsea_bar — bidirectional NES bar plot. .plot_gsea_ridge — ridge plot via     enrichplot::ridgeplot().

what: Rank Cell Types by Transcriptional Perturbation — RankPerturbation
title: Rank Cell Types by Transcriptional Perturbation — RankPerturbation
text: Measures much cell type's transcriptional state shifts two conditions DNN's learned embedding space. Cell types larger shifts ranked higher ("perturbed"). Inspired Augur using multi-task DNN representations.

title: Rank Cell Types by Transcriptional Perturbation — RankPerturbation
code: RankPerturbation(   embedding,   cell_meta,   cell_type_col = "cell_type_pred",   condition_col = "group",   conditions = NULL,   method = c("wasserstein", "mmd", "energy", "classifier"),   n_permutations = 500L,   min_cells = 10L,   n_pcs = 20L,   seed = 42L,   n_cores = 1L,   balance_cells = FALSE,   early_stop_alpha = 0.1,   verbose = TRUE )

title: Rank Cell Types by Transcriptional Perturbation — RankPerturbation
text: embedding Numeric matrix (n_cells x d), typically pred$shared_embedding DNN_predict(return_embedding = TRUE). Rownames must cell IDs. cell_meta data.frame (e.g. seurat_obj@meta.data). Rownames must cell IDs. cell_type_col Column name cell type labels (default "cell_type_pred"). condition_col Column name condition/group labels (default "group"). conditions Character vector length 2 specifying two conditions compare (e.g. c("PH", "SH")). NULL exactly 2 unique conditions exist, used automatically. method Distance metric: "wasserstein" (default, sliced Wasserstein distance random projections), "mmd" (Maximum Mean Discrepancy RBF kernel), "energy" (Energy distance, parameter-free), "classifier" (LDA-based AUC, Augur-like). n_permutations Number permutations p-value estimation (default 500). Set 0 skip. min_cells Minimum cells per cell-type-per-condition (default 10). n_pcs Number PCs dimensionality reduction distance computation (default 20). NULL skip PCA. seed Random seed (default 42). n_cores Number parallel cores computing across cell types. Default 1 (serial). Unix, uses parallel::mclapply; Windows, uses parallel::parLapply. balance_cells Logical. TRUE, downsample larger condition group match smaller one per cell type distance computation (default FALSE). early_stop_alpha Numeric. p-value threshold adaptive early stopping permutation tests. Permutations stop early running p-value clearly 2 * early_stop_alpha (default 0.1). Set 0 disable early stopping. verbose Logical. Print progress messages (default TRUE).

title: Rank Cell Types by Transcriptional Perturbation — RankPerturbation
text: named list: results data.frame: cell_type, score, p_value, p_adj,       n_cells_cond1, n_cells_cond2, rank. method Method used. conditions Two conditions compared.

title: Rank Cell Types by Transcriptional Perturbation — RankPerturbation
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ pred <- DNN_predict(query, model_path, return_embedding = TRUE) q1 <- Seurat::AddMetaData(toy_test, pred$predictions)  # Sliced Wasserstein (default) res <- RankPerturbation(pred$shared_embedding, q1@meta.data,                         conditions = c("PH", "SH"))  # Energy distance (fast, parameter-free) res_e <- RankPerturbation(pred$shared_embedding, q1@meta.data,                           conditions = c("PH", "SH"),                           method = "energy")  # With parallel computation res_p <- RankPerturbation(pred$shared_embedding, q1@meta.data,                           conditions = c("PH", "SH"),                           n_cores = 4)  print(res$results) PlotPerturbation(res) } # }

title: Run Differential Expression Test — RunDE
text: seu Seurat object. group.Column name meta.data grouping. NULL, uses Idents(seu). split.Optional column name second grouping variable (e.g. condition). provided, cells grouped group.x split.composite identity DE testing. Result columns celltype condition added. Default: NULL. group1 Character vector group labels first group. group2 Character vector group labels second group. cells1 Character vector cell barcodes first group. Overrides group1. cells2 Character vector cell barcodes second group. features Character vector genes test. Default: genes. markers_type Type marker analysis. One "" (one-vs-rest), "paired" (pairwise), "conserved" (across conditions), "disturbed" (condition-specific). grouping.var Column condition variable (required conserved/disturbed). meta.method Method combining p-values conserved markers. One "maximump", "minimump", "wilkinsonp", "meanp", "sump", "votep". test.use DE test method. Passed Seurat::FindMarkers. .pos Logical; return positive markers. Default: TRUE. fc.threshold Fold change threshold (linear scale, >= 1). Pre-filter testing: genes threshold skipped. Default: 1.5. p_val_cutoff Numeric NULL. Post-filter: keep results p_val_adj < p_val_cutoff. Default: NULL (filtering). logfc_cutoff Numeric NULL. Post-filter: keep results |avg_log2FC| > logfc_cutoff. Default: NULL (filtering). base Log base fold change. Default: 2. pseudocount.use Pseudocount log fold change. Default: 1. mean.fxn Custom mean function fold change. min.pct Minimum fraction cells expressing gene. Default: 0.1. min.diff.pct Minimum difference fraction groups. max.cells.per.ident Maximum cells per identity testing. latent.vars Latent variables certain tests (MAST, LR, etc.). min.cells.feature Minimum cells expressing feature. Default: 3. min.cells.group Minimum cells per group. Default: 3. norm.method Normalization method. Default: "LogNormalize". p.adjust.method P-value adjustment method. Default: "bonferroni". downsample Integer NULL. specified, downsample identity class many cells DE testing. Uses subset(seu, downsample = downsample). Default: NULL. layer Data layer use. Default: "data". assay Assay use. Default: DefaultAssay(seu). cores Integer. Number parallel cores. Default: parallel::detectCores() %/% 2 (half available cores). title: Run Trajectory GSEA Along Pseudotime — RunTraceGSEA
text: data.frame compatible PlotPathwayBubble,   columns: Celltype Lineage name (PlotPathwayBubble       x-axis compatibility). Lineage Lineage name. Pathway Pathway name (cleaned       clean.names = TRUE). Score NES (Normalized Enrichment Score). Positive =       pathway activated along trajectory; negative = repressed. PValue Raw p-value. AdjPValue BH-adjusted p-value. QValue Q-value. Sign "Activated" (NES > 0)       "Repressed" (NES < 0). SetSize Number genes set. EnrichmentScore Raw enrichment score. CoreEnrichment Core enrichment genes       (slash-separated). RankingMetric Ranking method used       ("rho" "signed_logp"). NCells Number cells lineage. Method "GSEA".

title: Run Trajectory GSEA Along Pseudotime — RunTraceGSEA
text: workflow lineage : Subset cells finite pseudotime values (.finite()). Use genes default (recommended GSEA); use HVG     features = "HVG". Filter genes minimum expression percentage (min.pct). Compute Spearman correlation (cor.test(method = "spearman"))     gene's expression pseudotime. Build gene ranking correlation statistics. Run clusterProfiler::GSEA() ranking. Two ranking metrics available: "rho" Uses Spearman rho directly. Genes positively     correlated pseudotime rank high (expression increases along     trajectory); negatively correlated rank low. mode uses     vectorized stats::cor() speed. "signed_logp" Uses -log10(p) * sign(rho)     sharper separation highly significant correlations. mode     calls cor.test() per gene slower provides     stronger discrimination.

title: Run Trajectory GSEA Along Pseudotime — RunTraceGSEA
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ library(scMMR)  # Hallmark gene sets gmt <- system.file("extdata", "gmt",   "h.all.v2022.1.Hs.symbols.gmt", package = "scMMR")  # Single lineage res <- RunTraceGSEA(seu, lineages = "Lineage1", gene.sets = gmt) PlotPathwayBubble(res)  # Multiple lineages with signed_logp ranking res <- RunTraceGSEA(   seu,   lineages = c("Lineage1", "Lineage2", "Lineage3"),   gene.sets = gmt,   ranking.metric = "signed_logp" ) PlotPathwayBubble(res, top.n = 15, facet.by.sign = TRUE)  # KEGG pathways with custom gene list kegg_gmt <- system.file("extdata", "gmt",   "c2.cp.kegg.v2022.1.Hs.symbols.gmt", package = "scMMR") res <- RunTraceGSEA(   seu,   lineages = "Lineage1",   gene.sets = kegg_gmt,   features = Seurat::VariableFeatures(seu),   min.pct = 0.05,   ranking.metric = "signed_logp" ) } # }

title: Find Pseudotime-Associated Genes Along Trajectories — RunTraceGene
text: seu Seurat object pseudotime stored meta.data. lineages Character vector column names seu@meta.data containing pseudotime values. Cells lineage NA. Multiple lineages analyzed independently. features Character vector gene names test. Default: NULL (use top n_candidates variable features). Set "" test genes. n_candidates Number variable features select features = NULL. Default: 2000. min.pct Minimum fraction lineage cells expressing gene (expression > 0). Default: 0.05. test.method Testing method: "spearman" (fast) "gam" (GAM smooth, rigorous). Default: "spearman". assay Seurat assay use. Default: DefaultAssay(seu). layer Data layer extract. Default: "data". smooth_k Number basis dimensions GAM smooth term (mgcv::s(k = ...)). used test.method = "gam". Smaller values = smoother curves. Default: 10. family Distribution family GAM. Default: "gaussian". Use "nb" raw count data (requires layer = "counts"). padjust.method Method p-value adjustment. Default: "fdr". verbose Logical; print progress messages. Default: TRUE.

title: Find Pseudotime-Associated Genes Along Trajectories — RunTraceGene
text: data.frame one row per gene-lineage combination: gene Gene name. lineage Lineage name. rho Spearman correlation coefficient (positive =       expression increases along pseudotime). pvalue Raw p-value (Spearman test GAM F-test). padjust Adjusted p-value. r_sq R-squared (GAM ; NA Spearman). dev_expl Deviance explained (GAM ; NA Spearman). peaktime Pseudotime peak expression (median       top 1% fitted/raw values). valleytime Pseudotime valley expression (median       bottom 1% fitted/raw values). exp_ncells Number cells expressing gene. direction "" (rho > 0) ""       (rho < 0). test_method Testing method used.

title: Find Pseudotime-Associated Genes Along Trajectories — RunTraceGene
text: lineage, function: Subsets cells finite pseudotime (.finite()). Selects candidate genes (HVG default, / user-specified). Filters genes minimum expression percentage (min.pct). Tests gene association pseudotime. Computes effect sizes, peak/valley times, adjusted p-values. Two test methods available: "spearman" Computes Spearman correlation gene     expression pseudotime. Fast (vectorized rho + per-gene p-value).     Output includes rho pvalue. "gam" Fits GAM smooth spline via mgcv::gam()     tests whether smooth term significant (F-test).     Output includes r_sq (R-squared), dev_expl     (deviance explained), pvalue. Additionally computes     Spearman rho direction. Requires mgcv.

title: Find Pseudotime-Associated Genes Along Trajectories — RunTraceGene
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ library(scMMR)  # Fast Spearman correlation (default) res <- RunTraceGene(seu, lineages = c("Lineage1", "Lineage2")) sig <- res[res$padjust < 0.05, ] head(sig[order(sig$padjust), ], 20)  # GAM fitting (more rigorous) res_gam <- RunTraceGene(seu, lineages = "Lineage1",                          test.method = "gam") sig_gam <- res_gam[res_gam$padjust < 0.05, ]  # Visualize top up-regulated genes up_genes <- head(sig$gene[sig$direction == "up"], 10) PlotDynamicFeatures(seu, pseudotime = "Lineage1", features = up_genes)  # Test all genes res_all <- RunTraceGene(seu, lineages = "Lineage1", features = "all") } # }
